violations.description: Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Different species of raw animal food was not properly separated from each other or arranged according to minimum cooking temperatures. Raw fish filets being stored directly on top of raw chicken in the drawer below the flattop. Raw chicken being stored above raw beef in the drawer below the flattop.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. Several wiping clothes were left out in the dish pit and prep areas.--In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. Ice scoop being stored on top of the ice machine.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Multiple containers throughout the facility were not date marked and could not be verified when they were made.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Green rack in the cooler, and shelves on the cook line need to be cleaned.

Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitize solution for mechanical warewashing machine was not maintained at the proper concentration. Sanitizer was not working on the dish machine in the dish pit.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. Single-use items being stored on the floor in the upstairs storage.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. Wet mop stored in the mop sink and not placed in a position to dry properly.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Secondary container of poisonous or toxic material was not clearly identified to indicated contents. Unlabeled chemical spray bottles in the dish pit.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 140F or above during display or service. Rice being stored on top of the oven, employees did not know how long it had been there and it was temping at 115 degrees.
Laundered, proper handling, and storage (1 pt deducted)
Clean, employee dressing area, cleaning equipment storage, living/sleeping quarters separate (1 pt deducted)
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Clean, proper use, storage, sanitizing solution (2 pts deducted)
Hot holding and cold holding temperatures maintained, unwrapped food not reserved (5 pts deducted)
Method, temperature and time (5 pts deducted)
Fruits and vegetables washed, handling, sink provided (1 pt deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
